steps.title: Test predator response
steps.content: Bell and colleagues claim the caterpillar sounds are directed primarily at predators. The strongest support would show that actual caterpillar predators respond to those sounds in a way that affects attacks.

steps.title: Reject weaker sound details
steps.content: A concerns whether different species make different sounds, not whether predators respond. C only partly addresses detectability and excludes whistles. D concerns consistency within species. Evidence that predators stop attacking after hearing caterpillar sounds directly supports the claim. The answer is B.
